title: Midcentury Bar Console Cabinet
summary: A plywood credenza on splayed midcentury-modern feet — three drawers side by side up top, and a single wide door below covering open storage for bottles and glassware.
description:
The carcass is built from wide-ripped 3/4" plywood: two full-height sides and three horizontal panels — top, a fixed mid-shelf that also divides the drawer row from the door bay below, and the bottom. Attach the four midcentury-modern feet to the bottom panel before standing the case up, since they're much easier to get level and square while the case is still on its side.

**The upper bay holds three drawers running side by side on euro-style bottom-corner-mount slides**, each a plain plywood box with a 1x8 face (or scrap plywood if that's what's on hand) — get the slides mounted level before building the drawers, since a slide that's off by even a little binds a drawer for good. The lower bay is left open behind a **single wide door**, hung on euro-style inset hinges, which is the concealed, easy-to-mount option that keeps the midcentury look clean from the front.

The 1x2 trim frames the top and sides of the case, and a plywood back panel closes the whole thing in if you want a finished back rather than an open one against the wall.

materials.name: Euro-style white bottom corner mount drawer slides
Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or wood screws, 2"
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the carcass
steps.body: Assemble the sides with the top, mid-shelf, and bottom panels into a two-bay case.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or wood screws, 2"

steps.title: Attach the feet
steps.body: Attach the four midcentury-modern feet to the bottom panel while the case is still on its side.

steps.title: Add the trim and back
steps.body: Attach the 1x2 trim around the top and sides, then nail on the plywood back panel.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Mount the three sets of euro-style drawer slides level in the upper bay.
materials: Euro-style white bottom corner mount drawer slides

steps.title: Build the drawers and door
steps.body: Assemble the three drawer boxes from their sides, front/back, and bottoms, attach the drawer faces, and build the single door panel for the lower bay.
materials: Pocket hole screws, 1-1/4", or wood screws, 2"

steps.title: Hang the door and add hardware
steps.body: Hang the door on euro-style inset hinges, then attach knobs or handles to the drawers and door.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Midcentury modern bar console with three drawers and a door
